biography_with_entities.raw_text:
ΛBЯΣᄃΛMIПӨƧ Philos༄.°Arte
Walking the pathless path 
ʎɐʍ ʎʇnɐǝq ǝɥ⊥
@portal__poem

node.text:
I live somewhere in between.

A fever,
the new horizon through the window,
the old white horse down at the river.
Sitting on a velvet couch, hazy stare,
babbling baby, hairy dog —

I can hear the caged roosters by the neighbor field,
trained to fight, they for sure know how to sing.
From the green hills my old life is a chimera made out of sand.

The white jeep, driving through timelines, spaceship.
Always a child at my breast, a burning heart, boiling milk, missing blood.

Dust, only dust stays. The heat like a cage,
the color of the wood and the feeling of my ribs against the bedsheets.

We stop at this oriental-palace-looking motel
but the girl next door laughs too loud,
my attention as always dragged away,

as I live somewhere in between
deep primal fear for the flesh of my flesh
and desire for an eternal silence.

A full moon at midnight.
Travelling ants came through our jungle home,
biting and killing.
We had to run away. You were poisoned.
Breathless. Fearful.

My bones have held two bodies and countless lives.
Some build, and I —
I became a master at undoing, at restarting.
There is truly nothing like a beginning.

When you’re somewhere in between,
nowhere to be going,
the land of possibles is where I live.
Formless and masterpiece, the shell still
hiding the seed, holding paradoxes at its core.

The liminal is my ocean, and I become mermaid.
Mermaids don’t sit.

No shelter for the ones that live on the edge.

node.text:
Our love so wild.
To the massive initiations we’ve been through together. 

We are saying goodbye to the sweet life by the ocean
For other horizons. It was very unexpected and sudden changes, and we are still holding the family through it all. 

Answering the invitation to let go of what we were attached to and what we had built, yet another rebirth !

We did this thing where I took your name in a small dusty office crowded with piles of paperwork and old writing machines. Lyra was wearing her swim goggles and asking “are those hu-mans?” all the while pointing at the people standing in line. I had to hush her upset in the bathroom cause she was throwing a tantrum to hold the camera, while the assigned government officer tried to read “do you want to become his wife?” I looked at my gorgeous husband across the room, hands too busy with babies. I thought: I’ve read so many magical realism novels that I ended up living in one.

We drove back home on the sunstroke highway and bought a mirror on the way, a big one made of parota. The heart-shaped one called to me first, but it was cracked and too distorted.

I wanted to officialise our union in front of my important people: the wind, the ocean, a big setting sun, clouds and sand, a bunch of stray dogs, and an altar made out of seeds and flowers. We tied a blue string across our hips but that made you feel trapped and anxious, so we broke it, and then we received blessings and mezcal, hot chocolate and dates, honey to wrap our marriage in eternal sweet madness.

I love you @astralintegration 
Another year, another wedding.
Tlazohcamati.
May we be always true
and truer.​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​

Eternal gratitude to @tuutuumakamane for officiating this beautiful ceremony & @ayakhnina for the photos that I will cherish forever & @verderame_preziosi_irene for the marvelous rings

Am I dreaming ?? I wasn’t set up for this life but somehow my soul knew that I would break all expectations. I never imagined I would have two babies, and be in love with a beautiful man. Not pictured the daily struggles, that an extra dose of humour and grace always manage to resolve. The family is growing in all directions, body, heart, spirit, non human beings. I’m losing my cynicism and criticism, and some days I start to manage to be really present with the beauty of it all.

node.text:
Today’s revolution
Is a body making a body
Is beauty
Today’s revolution is the breath of your beloved
landing in the cusp of your neck.
It’s a laughter breaking so loud
it stops the course of someone else’s thought.
Today’s revolution is a moment of doubt
swept away by a blossoming tree
in a sunlit city where colors bring old memories to life
and from the tip of eyelashes
a teardrop twinkles.
It’s a song on the radio becoming a time-travelling machine
as sugar melts on a pastry roll.
It’s the choice of patience when nerves are worked raw.
An exhale.
Today’s revolution is stumbling around
with a shameless little smile.
Caring the way a child plays.
Owning courage. Tracking self-importance.
Living as if it were already real.
Today’s revolution
Is your humanity
Is a body loving a body
Is beauty

#streamofconsciousness #visualdiary

I saw you standing behind my left shoulder,
a reassuring hand leaning next to my cheek,
wet with tears.
When I stare into your eyes
I finally feel the depth
of the unguarded heart of a man —
a man to be.
Deeply. Profoundly. Loving.
I saw you in my dreams
floating down a stream,
ready to change the world
with the strength of your beaming heart,
the containment of your divinity,
the groundlessness of your presence.
With you there are no doubts,
no questions —
just pure knowing.
I saw you in my dreams, tall and strong,
and already vibrating in your blood
there is this song of victory
defeating the old world —
all of this in a toothless smile.
The potential.
A tender warrior. A fierce lover.
I know all the mothers —
mothers of sons —
learn to love men there,
in the middle of an embrace
on a languid afternoon,
falling asleep swollen with pride
as they look down
at that precious profile.
Remember:
all men come from mothers.
All remnants of hatred
toward the penetrative force of nature
must be crucified
to raise a boy who grows into a man.
A hunter. A pioneer. A dreamer
who shapes ambition into reality.
How determined you are to achieve —
life force in action.
I saw you in my dreams
breaking into a million pieces
my heavy shield,
showing me the innocence and purity
in the heart of a man.
A boy.
A child of God.

#motherhoodjourney

📸 @somaticportraits

Am I falling or am I floating 
Maybe dancing, in a dark sky in which my body becomes a vessel for life
A giant void spits me out, I am tracking what’s left of myself when I’m not holding, bearing. 
Sometimes, the inside of my chest being turned outwards, a fish being sacrificed. 
I scream but you can’t hear, and I let myself go, maybe cormorants will come down to feed on my remnants.
I am insolent, to the face of life. I know how to be dragged in the mud and come out with a renewed sense of wonder. None of the collapses can pull me back from defiantly knowing that we’re tearing apart an old beast and on her last cries we will rise.
Am I falling or am I floating 
It’s not that it matters, if the ground becomes water. Only that there is this drive to know. Why? Time always tells . There is no escape from reality, truly. When will we be done loosing ourselves for a mirage. can we start using the outrage to grow flowers that feed on greed and fear ?
Make our existence a revolution
Our soul a manifest 
Our heart 
Poetry

@somaticportraits 📸

The morning caffè takes my sleep before I’ve even finished the cup. I’ve stopped fighting it.

Lately I move through the day like I’m collecting things — the way light breaks on the face of my loved ones, a stranger’s laugh that sounds like someone I used to care for, that makes my chest do something I don’t have words for yet. I’ve been out of the writing but deep in the material. Storing it all somewhere below language.

New friends that felt like remembering. The kind of answered prayer you almost miss because it arrives so quietly, dressed like an ordinary Tuesday.

And the feminine heart — god, I’d forgotten how much lives there. How much I’d locked out of my own.
The rage came first. It always does. Months of it sitting in my sternum like a stone I’d learned to breathe around. Then one night the mushrooms opened something —like a door kicked off its hinges. The gates of whatever I’d been guarding swung open and what came through wasn’t chaos. It was current. Just — current. Moving again.

Now, I am taking holidays from my holiday life. a hotel room watching bambi, even if my kids are napping . A bowl of pasta going cold because I keep forgetting to eat it. The aircon making everything feel like a snow globe.

Sitting with myself the way you sit with someone you’ve hurt and are slowly, carefully, making it right. Just the actual awkward tender work of loving oneself— the postpartum reinvention, the body and soul renegotiating their relationship 

I think I might be falling back in love. With the whole strange project of being alive.

node.text:
Softness of motherhood 

That day I came to my friend @kuraterra with heavy heart and pain in my shoulder. She held me with such tender love, offering massage and tea, flowers, embraced me in a bubble of adoration. She captured the deep wonder of starting into my baby’s eyes.

The love that women share with eachother is such a blessing. The resilience, the strength, the inherent beauty,

Women, we are healing eachother with presence. There will always be a sister, a mother, a friend, to guide us back to ourselves 

#motherhood #love #sisterhood #streamofconsciousness

Life lately ..
Indoor outdoor Shelters
Extreme motherhood 
Rythm & flow 
Beach naps 
New friendships 
Generational transmissions 
Backward horses 
Breakthroughs & breakdowns
